item.title: Morocco edge Scotland after record-breaking early strike
item.description: An early goal from Ismael Saibari proved decisive as Morocco secured a 1-0 victory over Scotland in their FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C encounter. Morocco got off to the perfect start when Saibari found the back of the net just one minute and 11 seconds after kickoff, giving his side an advantage they would hold onto for the remainder of the match.
item.content:encoded:
An early goal from Ismael Saibari proved decisive as Morocco secured a 1-0 victory over Scotland in their FIFA World Cup 2026 Group C encounter.

Morocco got off to the perfect start when Saibari found the back of the net just one minute and 11 seconds after kickoff, giving his side an advantage they would hold onto for the remainder of the match.

The goal is the fastest scored at the tournament so far, although it remains well short of the all-time FIFA World Cup record of 10.8 seconds, set by Turkey’s Hakan Şükür against South Korea in 2002.

Scotland struggled to break through Morocco’s disciplined defence and failed to find an equaliser, allowing the North African side to claim all three points.

Morocco entered the match on the back of a 1-1 draw with Brazil in their opening Group C fixture, while Scotland had begun their campaign with a narrow 1-0 win over Haiti.

The victory keeps Morocco firmly in contention for a place in the knockout stages. The Atlas Lions are making their seventh FIFA World Cup appearance, with their best performance coming in 2022 when they became the first African nation to reach the semi-finals.

Scotland, appearing at their ninth FIFA World Cup, are still chasing their first appearance beyond the group stage after exiting in the opening round in each of their previous eight campaigns.

Morocco will face Haiti in their final Group C match next Thursday at 10am, while Scotland take on Brazil at the same time in a crucial fixture that could determine their tournament fate.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Emotional return and homecoming for Verebalavu
item.description: BRUMBIES women prop Iris Verebalavu is relishing the opportunity to return home and face familiar faces when her side takes on the McDonald’s Fijian Drua Women at 2pm today at 4R Stadium in Ba. The 27-year-old, who hails from Namena in Tailevu, is one of two Fijians in the Brumbies squad for today’s Super Rugby Women’s clash and says coming back to Fiji with her teammates has been a special experi
item.content:encoded:
BRUMBIES women prop Iris Verebalavu is relishing the opportunity to return home and face familiar faces when her side takes on the McDonald’s Fijian Drua Women at 2pm today at 4R Stadium in Ba.

The 27-year-old, who hails from Namena in Tailevu, is one of two Fijians in the Brumbies squad for today’s Super Rugby Women’s clash and says coming back to Fiji with her teammates has been a special experience.

“I’m very much looking forward to it. I’m happy to be home and I’m glad to be here with my team. There are a lot of girls who have never been to Fiji before, so everyone’s quite excited going into the game today,” Verebalavu said.

“I love all my teammates in the other team. We all keep in contact quite often, so it’ll be a good challenge. I know how a lot of them play and vice versa, so I’m up for it and I know the team are as well.”

Verebalavu expects another physical contest against the defending champions and believes the Brumbies must be ready for the Drua’s exciting brand of rugby.

“We obviously know that they like to play a fast game. Their offload game is strong and they are quite a physical team, so we’re up for the challenge and hopefully we can put on a good performance.”

The Brumbies will be aiming to spoil the Drua’s home outing, while Verebalavu hopes to enjoy a memorable return to Fiji in front of family, friends and local supporters.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Buna ‘bullet’ set to fire
item.description: THE McDonald’s Fiji Drua Women will look to continue their strong home form when they host the Brumbies at 4R Stadium in Ba today at 2pm, with exciting winger Atelaite Buna nicknamed “Bullet” set to be one of the key weapons on attack. Known for her blistering speed and sharp finishing ability, Buna is expected to bring spark to the Drua backline as the side looks to build on lessons learned from
item.content:encoded:
THE McDonald’s Fiji Drua Women will look to continue their strong home form when they host the Brumbies at 4R Stadium in Ba today at 2pm, with exciting winger Atelaite Buna nicknamed “Bullet” set to be one of the key weapons on attack.

Known for her blistering speed and sharp finishing ability, Buna is expected to bring spark to the Drua backline as the side looks to build on lessons learned from their previous outing against the Western Force.

Speaking ahead of the match, Buna said the team has put in a solid week of preparation and is ready for another tough challenge.

“This week preparation, the girls have been preparing well for the game this Saturday and I’m also excited to have a run.

“It will be a good game and a tough match as well for us. We have improved and have worked on our weaknesses from the Western Force game.

“It will be a good game and we will prove ourselves.”

Head coach Mike Legge has also been pleased with the team’s response at training, highlighting their focus on fixing key areas such as breakdown work, defensive structure, and discipline after last week’s performance.

He said the Brumbies will bring a physical challenge and will aim to slow down the Drua’s attacking rhythm, but believes his side is better prepared this week.

“We’ve had a good week of preparation, the girls have worked hard on our weaknesses and we know what we need to improve.”

The match is expected to be another high-intensity contest, with the Drua Women looking to feed off the home crowd once again as they push for consistency across the full 80 minutes.

With pace out wide and growing confidence in camp, “Bullet” Buna could be the X-factor the Drua need to break open the Brumbies defence in Ba this afternoon.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Sea Eagles ready to defend league title
item.description: VODAFONE Cup men’s defending champions, the Namuaniwaqa Sea Eagles, are fired up and determined to retain their title in this year’s tournament. Their victory last year was the ultimate underdog tale, as they defied all expectations to win the competition in their debut season.
item.content:encoded:
VODAFONE Cup men’s defending champions, the Namuaniwaqa Sea Eagles, are fired up and determined to retain their title in this year’s tournament.

Their victory last year was the ultimate underdog tale, as they defied all expectations to win the competition in their debut season.

In a nail-biting 2025 grand final, they edged out the Vusu Raiders 32–30 in a thrilling golden-point showdown at the HFC Bank Stadium in Suva.

Captain Kelevi Balia revealed that preparations have been ramped up as the team gears up for their opening match this Saturday.

“We are ready to defend our title. We know it won’t be easy, but we’ve been training hard through the off-season and pre-season,” he said.

Being a village-based team, financial backing and access to proper training facilities have been significant challenges that the Sea Eagles have had to overcome.

“We’ve had to get creative with our training since we don’t have a proper gym in the village,” Balia explained.

“Our trainer has been making use of tyres, logs, the beach, and whatever else is available locally to help us reach the fitness levels needed for the competition.”

Comprising entirely of village youth, the team’s tight-knit bond has forged exceptional chemistry and cohesion on the field, which remains one of their biggest strengths.

“Two core values drive us: unity and faith,” said Balia.

“Unity so we can play seamlessly as one team and proudly represent our village, and faith to place our trust in the man above, guiding us both on and off the field.”

The Namuaniwaqa Sea Eagles will kick off their title defence by facing the Saru Dragons at 2pm in their season opener.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Tigers in sync
item.description: REWA staged a spirited second-half comeback to defeat Navua 2-1 in an entertaining Group A match of the 2026 BiC Fiji FACT at Subrail Park in Labasa last night. Navua began the contest brightly and looked the more dangerous side in the opening stages, creating several opportunities to break the deadlock. However, Rewa’s defence stood firm and managed to repel repeated attacks for most of the first
item.content:encoded:
REWA staged a spirited second-half comeback to defeat Navua 2-1 in an entertaining Group A match of the 2026 BiC Fiji FACT at Subrail Park in Labasa last night.

Navua began the contest brightly and looked the more dangerous side in the opening stages, creating several opportunities to break the deadlock. However, Rewa’s defence stood firm and managed to repel repeated attacks for most of the first half.

Just when it appeared both teams would head into the break level, Navua found the breakthrough in first-half injury time. Walter Leoru rose above the defence to head home and give his side a deserved 1-0 advantage.

Trailing at halftime, Rewa emerged with renewed determination in the second spell and quickly turned the match around. Tevita Waranivalu levelled the scores before Kavaia Rawaqa struck soon after to complete a stunning comeback and hand the Delta Tigers a 2-1 lead.

Navua pushed hard in search of an equaliser during the closing stages, but Rewa’s defence remained resolute to secure all three points.

In earlier matches, Ba and Lautoka both recorded convincing 4-1 victories over Nadroga and Nasinu respectively.

Action continues today with Nadroga facing Nasinu at noon, Rewa taking on Suva at 2pm, Ba meeting Lautoka at 4pm, and hosts Labasa playing Navua at 6pm.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Ba cruise to win in FACT opener
item.description: BA produced a dominant second-half performance to defeat Nadroga 4-1 in the second match of Day One of the 2026 BiC Fiji FACT at Subrail Park in Labasa yesterday. After a quiet and uneventful first half, the Men in Black came out firing after the break and quickly took control of the contest.
item.content:encoded:
BA produced a dominant second-half performance to defeat Nadroga 4-1 in the second match of Day One of the 2026 BiC Fiji FACT at Subrail Park in Labasa yesterday.

After a quiet and uneventful first half, the Men in Black came out firing after the break and quickly took control of the contest.

The breakthrough came courtesy of John Alick, who stunned spectators with a spectacular 35-yard strike to give Ba a deserved lead.

Buoyed by the opener, Ba continued to press forward and doubled their advantage just minutes later through Ryan Naresh.

Nadroga struggled to contain Ba’s attacking momentum as Etonia Dogalau found the back of the net twice, extending the lead to 4-0 and effectively putting the match beyond reach.

The Stallions’ hopes of mounting a comeback suffered a major setback when star striker Jibola Afonja was shown a red card following an off-the-ball incident, reducing Nadroga to 10 men.

Despite the numerical disadvantage, Nadroga continued to fight and were rewarded late in the match when Kehinde Adejumo scored a consolation goal. His strike denied Ba a clean sheet but did little to alter the outcome as the Men in Black cruised to a convincing 4-1 victory.

The result gives Ba a strong start to their Fiji FACT campaign, while Nadroga will need to regroup quickly ahead of their next pool match as they look to keep their tournament hopes alive.

In matches today, Ba plays Lautoka at 4pm while Nadroga takes on Nasinu at 12pm.

Ba: Isikeli Sevanaia, Praneel Naidu, Ilisoni Logaivou, Patrick Joseph, John Orobulu, Nabil Begg, Suliano Tawanakoro, Etonia Dogalau, John Alick, Ryan Naresh, David Supa

Nadroga: Sakeo Tageneca, Shaun Singh, Semesa Sacere, Ravinesh Reddy, Abdul Farid, Joesl Chand, Jone Ralumu, William Khan, Jibola Afonja, Blessed Francis, Kehinde Adejumo

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Blues start in style
item.description: LAUTOKA made a commanding start to its 2026 BiC Fiji FACT campaign with an emphatic 4-1 victory over Nasinu at Subrail Park yesterday. The Blues wasted little time asserting their dominance, with striker Veleni Rasorewa scoring twice in the opening stages of the match. Rasorewa capitalised on a defensive error to net his first goal before producing a brilliant solo effort to double Lautoka’s advan
item.content:encoded:
LAUTOKA made a commanding start to its 2026 BiC Fiji FACT campaign with an emphatic 4-1 victory over Nasinu at Subrail Park yesterday.

The Blues wasted little time asserting their dominance, with striker Veleni Rasorewa scoring twice in the opening stages of the match. Rasorewa capitalised on a defensive error to net his first goal before producing a brilliant solo effort to double Lautoka’s advantage.

Lautoka continued to pile on the pressure, and Rusiate Matererega added a third goal to put the side firmly in control. Nasinu managed to pull one back through Bruce Hughes, but Lautoka still enjoyed a comfortable 3-1 lead at halftime.

Matererega completed his brace in the second half, finding the back of the net once again to seal an impressive 4-1 win for the Blues and secure three valuable points in their opening fixture.

Despite the convincing scoreline, Lautoka team manager Banishal Naidu believes there is room for improvement.

“It was a good win for the team, but we missed several scoring opportunities. Finishing is an area we need to work on before our next match,” Naidu said.

Nasinu captain Maikah Dau accepted the defeat and remained optimistic about his team’s chances of progressing.

“It wasn’t our day. There were a number of things we planned to do that we couldn’t execute. However, we still have a chance, and our next game is a must-win for us,” Dau said.

Lautoka will face Ba at 4pm today, while Nasinu takes on Nadroga at noon in a crucial encounter.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Clicks for cancer support
item.description: DRIVEN by a deep passion for photography and a genuine desire to give back to the community, N Studio Fiji Printlab Photography has always sought meaningful ways to use its craft for a greater purpose. “Photography is more than just images, it’s about telling stories that matter,” says owner Mr. Navneet Narayan.
item.content:encoded:
DRIVEN by a deep passion for photography and a genuine desire to give back to the community, N Studio Fiji Printlab Photography has always sought meaningful ways to use its craft for a greater purpose.

“Photography is more than just images, it’s about telling stories that matter,” says owner Mr. Navneet Narayan.

Recognizing the vital work carried out by the Fiji Cancer Society, Mr. Narayan understood their need for support.

“We were mindful not to become a financial burden, so we chose to assist by offering our services pro bono,” he explains.

Through high–quality visuals, Printlab has helped the Society share important information about cancer and other health–related issues across Fiji.

One of the most rewarding aspects of this partnership has been seeing these images used to support the Fiji Cancer Society in achieving its fundraising goals.

Among the most significant of these is the annual Charity Golf Tournament, a flagship event that brings together businesses, sports enthusiasts, and families to raise funds for patient care and awareness programs.

For Mr. Narayan, capturing the tournament is more than documenting a sporting event, it is about preserving moments of unity, compassion, and hope.

“Every swing on the course represents more than a game; it’s a step toward better health and stronger communities,” he reflects.

The visuals produced by N Studio Fiji Printlab not only highlight the excitement of the tournament but also amplify its deeper purpose: promoting cancer awareness and encouraging preventive health measures, particularly among men.

Men’s health remains a critical focus for the Fiji Cancer Society, with prostate, testicular, and bowel cancers often diagnosed late due to limited awareness and reluctance to seek early screening.

By linking the Charity Golf Tournament to men’s health advocacy, Printlab’s imagery helps spark conversations that might otherwise remain unspoken. “If our photos can inspire one man to get screened or one family to support awareness, then we know our work has made a difference,” Mr. Narayan shares.

Looking ahead, N Studio Printlab Photography hopes to continue supporting the Fiji Cancer Society in every efficient way possible.

“We encourage other corporate organizations to contribute as well, together, we can empower our communities to better combat health challenges,” he says.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.
